features.name: Product Tour V2 — Complete Bugfix & Wow-Factor Enhancement
features.description: Fix the completely broken product tour (black screen, invisible buttons, stacking overlay) and enhance it into a wow-factor onboarding experience. Show off V1 features (Dashboard, Meetings, Intelligence, Integrations, Credits) with a temporary fake demo meeting that showcases meeting analysis capabilities (transcript, AI summary, sentiment, talk time). Fake meeting data is injected client-side only and cleaned up when the tour ends.

stories.title: Fix black screen — remove SVG background-color override
acceptance: Remove `.driver-overlay { background-color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important; }` from tour.css — this sets background on an SVG element, creating a solid black layer that hides the evenodd spotlight cutout
Overlay color and opacity are controlled ONLY via driver.js config props (`overlayColor`, `overlayOpacity`) in ProductTour.tsx — NOT via CSS
Spotlight cutout (transparent hole around highlighted element) is visible and working
Overlay is semi-transparent (overlayOpacity ~0.5) — page content visible through it
Tour launches, highlights [data-tour='dashboard'] sidebar item with visible spotlight on first step

stories.title: Fix button visibility and popover styling
acceptance: Next button: solid violet-600 bg (#7c3aed), white text, 8px 18px padding, rounded-lg, 600 font-weight, hover darkens to violet-700
Previous button: subtle rgba(255,255,255,0.08) bg, gray-200 text, 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.12) border, hover brightens
Close (×) button: visible gray-400, hover brightens to white with subtle bg
Progress text ('3 of 10') visible in gray-500
Verify CSS selectors match driver.js v1.4.0 class names (check actual DOM elements in browser DevTools)
Popover glassmorphic shell renders correctly: dark bg, violet glow border, backdrop blur

stories.title: Create tour demo meeting data and TourMeetingDetail page
acceptance: New file `src/components/tour/tourDemoData.ts` — contains a fake UnifiedMeeting for the list view and full meeting detail data (reuse/adapt demoMeetings[0] from src/components/demo/data/meetings.ts)
Fake meeting: 'DataFlow Systems — Platform Demo', 52 min, sentiment 0.72, coach rating 8, talk time 38%, type 'demo', 3 open tasks
Full transcript text in [HH:MM:SS] Speaker: text format (12+ realistic exchanges showing sales conversation)
AI summary with key topics, action items, buyer signals, and next steps
Sentiment reasoning text explaining the 0.72 score
New file `src/pages/TourMeetingDetail.tsx` — static meeting detail page that renders same layout as MeetingDetail but with hardcoded data (no Supabase queries)

TourMeetingDetail shows: video placeholder area (dark bg with play icon + 'Recording available after your first meeting'), sentiment card, talk time chart, transcript tab with color-coded speakers, summary tab
Route `/meetings/tour-demo` registered in router BEFORE the `:id` wildcard route
data-tour='meeting-detail' attribute on the TourMeetingDetail outer container
data-tour='meeting-transcript' attribute on the transcript section
data-tour='meeting-analysis' attribute on the sentiment/talk-time section

stories.title: Inject fake demo meeting into UnifiedMeetingsList during tour
acceptance: Create a minimal Zustand store `src/lib/stores/tourStore.ts` with `isTourActive: boolean` flag and `setTourActive(active: boolean)` action
ProductTour.tsx sets `tourStore.setTourActive(true)` when tour starts, `setTourActive(false)` in handleTourEnd
UnifiedMeetingsList reads `tourStore.isTourActive` — when true, prepends the fake TOUR_DEMO_MEETING to the displayed list
Fake meeting row appears at the top of the meetings list with a subtle shimmer/glow border or '60 Demo' badge to make it stand out
When tour ends, fake meeting disappears from the list immediately
No database writes — everything is client-side state only

stories.title: Redesign tour steps — wow-factor flow with meeting deep-dive
acceptance: Tour flow (13 steps): 1) Dashboard sidebar → 2) Dashboard overview metrics → 3) Meetings sidebar → 4) Meetings list (with fake meeting visible) → 5) Click into fake meeting detail → 6) Meeting analysis section (sentiment + talk time) → 7) Meeting transcript section → 8) Intelligence sidebar → 9) Intelligence dashboard → 10) Integrations sidebar → 11) Integrations grid → 12) Credits widget → 13) Final 'Let's Go' step
Step 5 navigates to /meetings/tour-demo and highlights [data-tour='meeting-detail']
Step 6 highlights [data-tour='meeting-analysis'] with tooltip: 'AI-Powered Analysis — Every meeting gets sentiment scoring, talk-time ratios, and coaching insights automatically'
Step 7 highlights [data-tour='meeting-transcript'] with tooltip: 'Smart Transcripts — Color-coded speakers, key moment detection, and searchable history across all your calls'
Each step has compelling, concise copy that sells the feature (not just describes it)
First step shows only Next + Close (no Previous), last step shows 'Let's Go' button text
Navigation between pages uses waitForElement with 3s timeout, graceful skip on failure

stories.title: Polish tour UX — smooth transitions, edge cases, final design pass
acceptance: Spotlight moves smoothly between elements (driver.js animate: true, no flash)
ESC key dismisses tour correctly, cleans up tour store state
Component unmount cleans up tour store state (setTourActive(false))
Tour handles missing elements gracefully (skips step, logs warning)
No console errors during complete tour flow
Tour works with both expanded and collapsed sidebar
After tour ends: navigates back to /dashboard, fake meeting removed from list, setup wizard opens (if completed all steps)
Verify overlayOpacity is comfortable — not too dark (can't see content) and not too light (doesn't draw attention to spotlight)
Popover arrow matches glassmorphic background color

parallelGroups.reason: Button CSS, demo meeting data/page, and list injection are independent — can run in parallel after TOURFIX-001

designNotes.rootCause: driver.js uses an SVG element with fill-rule='evenodd' to create the spotlight cutout. The CSS rule `.driver-overlay { background-color: rgb(0,0,0) !important }` sets a background on the SVG element itself, creating a solid black layer UNDERNEATH the SVG path. This black background does not participate in the evenodd fill-rule logic, so it completely hides the transparent spotlight hole. Fix: remove this CSS rule entirely and control overlay via driver.js config props only.
designNotes.driverJsOverlayMechanism: SVG with evenodd fill-rule — outer rectangle (viewport) + inner rounded rectangle (spotlight hole). The path intersection is transparent. Opacity and color are set via inline JS styles on the <path> element, NOT via CSS background-color on the <svg>.
designNotes.fakeMeetingStrategy: Client-side only. Zustand tourStore.isTourActive flag. UnifiedMeetingsList prepends a fake meeting when flag is true. TourMeetingDetail is a static page at /meetings/tour-demo that renders hardcoded meeting data (transcript, sentiment, talk time) with the same layout as MeetingDetail but no Supabase queries. On tour end, flag resets and fake data disappears.
tourFlow: 1. Sidebar: Dashboard → 2. Page: Dashboard overview metrics
3. Sidebar: Meetings → 4. Page: Meetings list (with demo meeting)
5. Page: Meeting detail (fake) → 6. Meeting analysis (sentiment/talk time)

tourCopy.dashboard: Your Command Centre — Track targets, team performance, and AI agent activity at a glance.
tourCopy.dashboardOverview: Real-Time Metrics — Revenue, outbound activity, meetings, and proposals. Set targets and watch them update live.
tourCopy.meetings: Meeting Intelligence — Every meeting gets an AI summary, action items, and follow-up draft automatically.
tourCopy.meetingsList: All Your Meetings — Recordings from every source appear here. Search, filter, and dive into any call.
tourCopy.meetingDetail: Deep Dive — Click into any meeting for the full story: recording, transcript, AI analysis, and next steps.
tourCopy.meetingAnalysis: AI-Powered Analysis — Sentiment scoring, talk-time ratios, and coaching insights from every call.
tourCopy.meetingTranscript: Smart Transcripts — Color-coded speakers, key moments flagged, and fully searchable across all calls.
tourCopy.intelligence: Analytics & Insights — Trends across all meetings: talk ratios, sentiment, and coaching recommendations.
tourCopy.intelligenceDashboard: Meeting Analytics — Track patterns over time and get AI coaching to improve your conversations.
tourCopy.integrations: Connect Your Tools — Link CRM, calendar, and email so 60 works across your entire workflow.
tourCopy.integrationsGrid: Your Integrations — Google Workspace, HubSpot, Slack, and more. Each unlock new AI capabilities.
tourCopy.credits: AI Credits — Each AI action uses credits. You start with free credits — earn more by completing setup.
